source_text: Upon receiving an error alert, the TLS implementation SHOULD indicate an error to the application and MUST NOT allow any further data to be sent or received on the connection.
rule_statement: Upon receiving an error alert, the implementation MUST NOT allow any further data to be sent or received.
checkability_rationale: Can verify no data is sent or accepted after error alert receipt.

source_text: Servers and clients MUST forget the secret values and keys established in failed connections, with the exception of the PSKs associated with session tickets, which SHOULD be discarded if possible.
rule_statement: On connection failure, endpoints MUST forget secret values and keys (except ticket PSKs which SHOULD be discarded).
expected_value: secrets and keys erased from memory
checkability_rationale: Internal memory management behavior; hard to verify externally.

source_text: All the alerts listed in Section 6.2 MUST be sent with AlertLevel=fatal and MUST be treated as error alerts when received regardless of the AlertLevel in the message.
rule_statement: All Section 6.2 alerts MUST be sent with AlertLevel=fatal and MUST be treated as errors regardless of received AlertLevel.
applies_when: Sending or receiving alerts listed in §6.2
expected_value: fatal (2) when sending; treated as error regardless when receiving
checkability_rationale: Can verify AlertLevel field value in sent alerts and handling of received alerts.

source_text: Unknown Alert types MUST be treated as error alerts.
rule_statement: Unknown alert types MUST be treated as error alerts (fatal).
applies_when: Endpoint receives an alert with unrecognized AlertDescription
checkability_rationale: Can send unknown alert type and verify receiver treats it as fatal.
